lede: A look at March 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ethan Nwaneri and Scott Eastwood, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: China Eastern Airlines Flight 5735
events.desc: China Eastern Airlines Flight 5735 crashes in Guangxi, China, killing 132 people.

events.title: 2019 Xiangshui chemical plant explosion
events.desc: The 2019 Xiangshui chemical plant explosion occurs, killing at least 47 people and injuring 640 others.

events.title: San Diego Comic-Con
events.desc: San Diego Comic-Con, the largest pop and culture festival in the world, hosts its inaugural event.

events.title: Los Angeles Rams
events.desc: The Los Angeles Rams sign Kenny Washington, making him the first African American player in professional American football since 1933.

events.title: List of monarchs of Iran
events.desc: Shah of Iran Reza Shah Pahlavi formally asks the international community to call Persia by its native name, Iran.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The first phase of the German spring offensive, Operation Michael, begins.

events.title: Alexander H. Stephens
events.desc: Alexander H. Stephens gives the Cornerstone Speech.

events.title: Baháʼí calendar
events.desc: The Baháʼí calendar begins. This is the first day of the first year of the Baháʼí calendar. It is annually celebrated by members of the Baháʼí Faith as the Baháʼí New Year or Náw-Rúz.

events.title: Napoleonic Wars
events.desc: Napoleonic Wars: Austrian forces repel French troops in the Battle of Arcis-sur-Aube.

events.title: Great New Orleans Fire (1788)
events.desc: A fire in New Orleans leaves most of the town in ruins.

births.name: Éric Rohmer
births.line: French director, film critic, journalist, novelist and screenwriter (died 2010)

deaths.text: Michael Lyster, Irish radio and television broadcaster
Chinua Achebe, Nigerian novelist, poet, and critic
Manolis Chiotis, Greek singer-songwriter and bouzouki player

observances.text: Christian feast day: Passing of Saint Benedict (Order of Saint Benedict, pre-1970 Calendar)
